title: Plato Alarm Rule
description: Schema for an alarm rule within a room configuration. Alarms define conditions that trigger automated responses.

id.description: Unique identifier for this alarm rule within the room. Must be snake_case.

condition.description:
Boolean expression that triggers the alarm when true.

Syntax:
- Reference sensor keys by their property name
- Comparison operators: >, <, >=, <=, ==, !=
- Logical operators: and, or
- Examples:
  - coolant_temp_c > 95
  - pressure_psi < 15
  - temp_c > 40 and humidity_pct > 80
  - level_a > 70 or level_b > 70

cooldown_sec.description:
Minimum number of seconds between consecutive triggers of this alarm.

- 0: triggers every tick when condition is true
- 5-30: typical for safety-critical alarms
- 300-3600: typical for informational/warning alarms

severity.description:
Severity level determines the response behavior.

- info: Log only, no notification. Used for status tracking.
- warning: Notify relevant personnel. Investigate when convenient.
- critical: Immediate notification, automatic mitigation actions.

actions.description:
List of actions to execute when the alarm triggers.

Actions are strings that map to handlers in the Plato engine.
Common actions include:
- log: Record the alarm event
- notify_*: Send notification to a role
- sound_alarm: Activate audible alarm
- activate_*: Enable an actuator
- shut_off_*: Disable an actuator
- *_door, *_pump, *_valve: Physical actions
